title: 15 Preparing Outgoing Wires
body_text:
15	Preparing Outgoing Wires
15.1	Overview of Preparing Outgoing Wires
The guidelines in this Chapter assist users preparing to enter outgoing wires in the Wires application. Use the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, to assist with record-keeping requirements and data entry. To ensure regulatory compliance and to mitigate risk, follow your financial institution芒聙聶s policies and procedures.
Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information.
Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part
for additional information.
Related Information
Outgoing Wire Request,
Form 1171
15.2	Ascertaining the Identity of the Wire Sender
Your financial institution must ascertain the identity of the individual initiating international or domestic outgoing wires for:
International or domestic outgoing wires of $1,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more
Foreign exchange wires of $3,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more, if the sender is a non-account holder, or if the sender is an account holder but the required information isn芒聙聶t on file
Financial institutions must establish policies and procedures to identify the sender and/or beneficiary, as applicable, for outgoing wires that are suspicious and must be reported on a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report; unless the individual was previously identified, or if doing so would tip off the sender that a report is being submitted.
Note:	FINTRAC has different requirements for determining the identify for a face-to-face transaction and a non-face-to-face transaction.
Important
:	Central 1 doesn芒聙聶t recommend that financial institutions permit non-account holders to send outgoing wires. Such transactions are unusual and high risk.
Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, includes a section to capture identification information.
Ensure that you identify the sender in accordance with
PCMLTFA
regulations. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information.
Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part
for additional information.
Related Information
Outgoing Wire Request,
Form 1171
15.3	Politically Exposed Person (PEP) / Head of International Organization (HIO) Determination
Your financial institution must take reasonable measures to determine if an individual sending an outgoing wire of $100,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more is a domestic or international politically exposed person (PEP), or head of an international organization (HIO). If the wire is made on behalf of a business, the determination must be made on the individual acting for the business.
You can record the PEP/HIO declaration on the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also use the Politically Exposed Person Statement, Form 2351.
Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information.
Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part
for additional information.
Related Information
Outgoing Wire Request,
Form 1171
Politically Exposed Person Statement,
Form 2351
(available to subscribers of the Operation Manual Program)
15.4	Completing the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171
Complete the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, before entering an outgoing wire to assist with
The collection of data for entry into the Wires application.
Information and record-keeping requirements for:
芒聢聮
Outgoing wires of $1,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more.
芒聢聮
Outgoing wires that require a foreign currency exchange.
Making a PEP/HIO determination for wires of $100,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more.
Obtaining the sender芒聙聶s authorization and acknowledgement that:
芒聢聮
They have fully disclosed any suspicious circumstances of any deposits on which the wire is based.
芒聢聮
They have consent to disclose personal information (as defined in applicable privacy legislation) about an individual included in information accompanying the wire.
芒聢聮
If the wire is based on an item that芒聙聶s later returned for any reason, the loss is to their account.
芒聢聮
The information they have provided is true and accurate.
芒聢聮
The outgoing wire is final once sent.
芒聢聮
Refunds aren芒聙聶t guaranteed.
芒聢聮
Charges may be deducted from a refund.
芒聢聮
Recalls may not be paid at the same rate of exchange as that on the sending date.
Important
:	Wire recalls, amendments, and trace requests are processed on a best-efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed. Once a wire has been released by Central 1, the receiving bank must voluntarily agree to return the funds. Central 1 will make every reasonable effort to support your request, however, the receiving institution may decline, delay, or be unable to return the funds.
Complete the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, as described. See Figure 1, Figure 2, and Figure 3. Fields with an asterisk (*) are required.
芒聭
Wire Amount and Currency
Record the amount of the outgoing wire in CAD. If the outgoing wire is sent in foreign funds and a currency exchange is required, the Canadian dollar equivalent can be entered in this field.
Date and Time
芒聙聯 Record the date and time that the outgoing wire is entered in Wires.
Future Date 芒聙聯
Record if the wire is future-dated. If yes, record the future date. For more information on future-dated wires, see Section 16.7, Completing the Future-Dated Wire Section, for regular wires, or Section 17.7, for bank-to-bank wires. Future-dated wires are subject to additional processing fees.
Wire Priority
芒聙聯 Indicate if the wire is high priority. Additional fees apply to high priority wires. For more information on priority wires, see Section 16.6, Completing the Wire Priority Section, for regular wires, or Section 17.6, for bank-to-bank wires. High priority wires are subject to additional processing fees.
Wire Currency
芒聙聯 Record the currency of the outgoing wire (e.g., CAD, USD, EUR). Select the Payable to Recipient Upon Identification check box if the beneficiary isn芒聙聶t a client of the receiving financial institution and funds are released upon presentation of the beneficiary芒聙聶s identification.
Sending Clerk Name
芒聙聯 Record the name of the sending clerk.
Wire Amount
芒聙聯 Record the amount of the outgoing wire in Canadian dollars. If the outgoing wire is sent in foreign funds and a currency exchange is required, enter the Canadian dollar equivalent.
US $ / Foreign Currency Amount
芒聙聯 If the outgoing wire is sent in foreign funds, record the foreign funds amount.
Exchange Rate
芒聙聯 Record the live exchange rate used when the wire is created, if applicable.
Receiving Commission
芒聙聯 Not applicable unless the sender wants to include the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s fees in the amount of the outgoing wire, and the amount of the fees is known.
Charges
芒聙聯 Record the sending financial institution芒聙聶s charge and the pass-through charge.
Other
芒聙聯 Record any other fees, if applicable.
Total
芒聙聯 Record the amount for which the sender will be debited.
芒聭隆
Sender Information
Financial Institution Details
芒聙聯 Record the name, transit number, full address, and phone number of the sending financial institution.
Sender Details
芒聙聯 Record the customer type, account and customer number, name, and full address of the sender (ordering customer).
Foreign Currency Exchange Transaction Details
芒聙聯 If the outgoing wire is purchased with a currency other than that in which it芒聙聶s sent, record the currency of purchase, the medium of exchange of purchase (e.g., cash, cheques), and the amount of purchase.
Additional Information for Non-Account Holder Outgoing Wires of $1,000 (Canadian Equivalent) or More
芒聙聯 If the outgoing wire is $1,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more, record the sender芒聙聶s occupation or nature of principal business, date of birth, phone number, identification type, reference number, expiration date, and place and country of issue. This information doesn芒聙聶t have to be recorded on the Wire Request if the sender is an account holder and the required information is recorded in the account documentation.
Wire Funds
芒聙聯 Record how the sender paid for the wire. Record if information about the source of funds (person/entity) was obtained. If yes, record how the requester initially obtained the funds.
Politically Exposed Person (PEP) Declaration
芒聙聯 Make a PEP/HIO determination on all wires equivalent to $100,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more within 14 days of sending the wire. The PEP determination must be made on individuals conducting transactions on personal accounts and on individuals conducting transactions on business accounts. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part for additional information.
Figure 1: Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, page 1
芒聭垄
Beneficiary Information
Financial Institution Details
芒聙聯 Record the name, routing information (transit number, ABA Number, or SWIFT), full address, and phone number of the receiving financial institution.
Beneficiary Details
芒聙聯 Record the customer type, name, full address, account number (or IBAN if applicable) of the beneficiary.
Note:	If the outgoing wire is directed to an IBAN-regulated country, obtain the IBAN from the sender and record it in the Account No. field. See Section 14.3, IBAN Requirements.
芒聭拢
Intermediary Financial Institution Information
Record the intermediary financial institution芒聙聶s SWIFT ID and, if known, the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s account number with the intermediary. Or record the name and address of the intermediary financial institution and, if known, the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s account number with the intermediary.
Important
:	You must obtain the intermediary information from the sender when sending Canadian dollars to the US. Record the intermediary information the sender provides on the form. You must search for and select the specified intermediary when creating the wire on the Wires application. Central 1 can芒聙聶t provide the intermediary information.
Note: 	For all other wires sent in the non-domestic currency of the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s country (e.g., Euros to the United Kingdom), select from the list of suggested intermediaries in the Wires application. The recommended intermediary is the default. You can record the intermediary selected on the form if applicable.
For wires sent in the domestic currency of the receiving financial institution芒聙聶s country (e.g., GBP to the United Kingdom), you aren芒聙聶t required to select an intermediary. Central 1芒聙聶s correspondent bank facilitates the routing of the wire to the receiving financial institution.
All international outgoing wires are processed through intermediary financial institutions that can deduct fees from the wire. Central 1 doesn芒聙聶t control the fees other financial institutions charge.
芒聭陇
Additional Payment Details
Complete this section to include additional information for the beneficiary (e.g., an invoice number).
Note:	Since special characters can芒聙聶t be entered on Wires, don芒聙聶t record them on the form.
芒聭楼
Sender Financial Institution to Receiver Financial Institution Information
Complete this section to include additional information for the beneficiary芒聙聶s financial institution.
Figure 2: Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, Page 2
芒聭娄
Additional Information for FINTRAC Reporting
Information recorded in this section can be included in the FINTRAC EFT Report (if applicable). Make a reasonable effort to complete this section if the outgoing wire is $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more.
If the financial institution is aware of multiple transfers conducted by the same person that, in total, equal or exceed $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) within 24 hours, the financial institution must report the wires to FINTRAC.
Note:	Your financial institution is responsible for analysis of the data and for reporting wires to FINTRAC.
Enter the information recorded on the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, in Wires as appropriate.
Note:	If your financial institution enrolls in the Wires Data Extract Service, the information is included in the data files and report files that Central 1 provides your financial institution. See Section 4.4, Wires Data Extract Service.
Sender (Ordering Customer) Details
芒聙聯 Make a reasonable effort to record the sender芒聙聶s phone number and, if the sender is an individual, their date of birth, occupation, ID type, and ID number. If the sender is an account holder, this information can be obtained from the account records.
Beneficiary Customer Details
芒聙聯 Make a reasonable effort to record the beneficiary芒聙聶s phone number and, if the beneficiary is an individual, their date of birth, occupation, and ID type.
Sender (Ordering Customer) Third Party Information and Beneficiary Customer Third Party Information
芒聙聯 Make a reasonable effort to determine whether a third party is involved in sending or receiving the outgoing wire. If a third party is identified, select the
Yes
radio button beside 芒聙聹Is this wire being sent on behalf of a third party?芒聙聺 and/or 芒聙聹Will this transfer be received on behalf of a third party?芒聙聺 Indicate if the third party is an individual or an entity. Make a reasonable effort to obtain the third party芒聙聶s name, address, date of birth (individuals only), occupation (individuals only), ID type (individuals only), and their relationship to the account holder.
芒聭搂
Sender Agreement
Ask the sender to sign the form and record the date.
Figure 3: Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, Page 3
Distribute the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, as follows:
Give a copy to the sender as a receipt.
Retain a copy for your records. The R5 code on the bottom right of page 1 of the form indicates the retention period is at least five years following the date of the transaction.
Related Information
Outgoing Wire Request,
Form 1171
Politically Exposed Person Statement,
Form 2351
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
15.5	Obtaining Payment for an Outgoing Wire
Obtain payment for an outgoing wire before entering it in the Wires application, because funds are guaranteed when sent. Ensure that the payment is made with cleared funds or cash. Additional requirements apply if an outgoing wire is paid with $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more in cash. Contact your compliance officer or FINTRAC for additional information. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part.
Note:	After an outgoing wire is successfully sent, there is no guarantee that it can be returned, even if it was sent in error. A recall can be requested, but it芒聙聶s processed on a best-efforts basis.
Important
:	Wire recalls, amendments, and trace requests are processed on a best-efforts basis and aren芒聙聶t guaranteed. Once a wire has been released by Central 1, the receiving bank must voluntarily agree to return the funds. Central 1 will make every reasonable effort to support your request, however, the receiving institution may decline, delay, or be unable to return the funds.
Record-keeping requirements apply if payment for the outgoing wire is made with a currency other than that in which it芒聙聶s sent. Record the foreign exchange transaction information in the space provided on the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171. See Section 15.4, Completing the Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, for more information. Alternatively, complete a separate foreign exchange transaction ticket. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can use the Foreign Exchange Transaction Ticket, Form 1923, and its Completion Instructions to assist with record keeping.
Related Information
Outgoing Wire Request,
Form 1171
Foreign Exchange Transaction Ticket,
Form 1923
(available to subscribers of the Operations Manual Program)
15.6	Record Keeping and FINTRAC EFT Reporting
Users must record accurate and complete originator information on all domestic and international outgoing wires. FINTRAC defines originator information as the name, address, and, if any, the account number or reference number of the individual requesting the wire.
Important
:	Wires can芒聙聶t verify the accuracy or completeness of the data you enter. Don芒聙聶t enter miscellaneous originator information to satisfy the mandatory field requirement in Wires.
Your financial institution must make a reasonable effort to collect additional information for wires equal to $10,000 (Canadian equivalent) or more and sent outside of Canada in any currency.
The additional information includes:
Sender Details
芒聢聮
Phone number
芒聢聮
Date of birth (individuals only)
芒聢聮
Occupation (individuals only)
芒聢聮
ID type and number (individuals only)
Beneficiary Details
芒聢聮
Phone number
芒聢聮
Date of birth (individuals only)
芒聢聮
Occupation (individuals only)
芒聢聮
ID type and number (individuals only)
Sender Third Party Information
芒聢聮
If the wire is being sent on behalf of a third party
芒聢聮
If the customer is an individual or entity
芒聢聮
Third party name
芒聢聮
Third party address
芒聢聮
Date of birth (individuals only)
芒聢聮
Occupation (individuals only)
芒聢聮
ID type and number (individuals only)
Beneficiary Third Party Information
芒聢聮
If the wire is being received on behalf of a third party
芒聢聮
If the customer is an individual or entity
芒聢聮
Third party name
芒聢聮
Third party address
芒聢聮
Date of birth (individuals only)
芒聢聮
Occupation (individuals only)
芒聢聮
ID type and number (individuals only)
Note:	The Outgoing Wire Request, Form 1171, includes a section to capture information for FINTRAC EFT reporting requirements.
Wires may request additional information about the sender for FINTRAC reporting purposes if it determines that the outgoing wire may be reportable on a FINTRAC EFT Report.
Note:	If you have configured Wires to always display FINTRAC fields, the additional information is collected for every outgoing wire. See Section 4.2, Description of Wires Customizable Elements.
Financial institutions are responsible for submitting reports to FINTRAC either directly or through a designated service provider.
Reporting Suspicious Outgoing Wires to FINTRAC
If there are reasonable grounds to suspect that an outgoing wire is related to a money laundering or terrorist financing offence, submit a FINTRAC Suspicious Transaction Report.
Caution
: 	A financial institution mustn芒聙聶t disclose to the individual that it will submit a FINTRAC Suspicious or Attempted Suspicious Transaction Report.
Reporting Terrorist Property to FINTRAC
Submit a FINTRAC Terrorist Property Report to FINTRAC if your financial institution knows or believes it has an outgoing wire in its possession or control that芒聙聶s owned or controlled by or on behalf of a terrorist, terrorist group, or listed person.
Proposed transactions must not be completed and any property in the financial institution芒聙聶s possession or control must be frozen.
Reporting International Outgoing Wires of $10,000 (Canadian Equivalent) or More to FINTRAC
Large international outgoing wires ($10,000 Canadian equivalent or more) must be reported to FINTRAC. This also applies if two or more transactions are sent within 24 hours and, in total, equal a large international outgoing wire and your financial institution knows that the transactions are conducted by or on behalf of the same person.
Financial institutions that send outgoing wires through Central 1 are responsible for reporting all outgoing international wires that meet reporting criteria to FINTRAC, whether conducted as a single transaction or multiple transactions. Subscribers to the Operations Manual Program can also refer to the PCMLTFA Compliance Part
for additional information.
Related Information
Outgoing Wire Request,
Form 1171
END
